records.ety: [L. cedere to withdraw, yield; akin to cadere to fall, and to E. chance; cf. F. cder.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. ceded; p. pr. & vb. n. ceding.]

defs: To yield or surrender; to give up; to resign; as, to cede a fortress, a province, or country, to another nation, by treaty.
quotes: The people must cede to the government some of their natural rights.
